rationale: Y catches the two concerns human reviewers emphasized most — the trust_remote_code default flip (near-verbatim match to alex-jw-brooks's inline comment) and the endpoint-restriction/deploy-override resolution order, correctly noting the regression test now covers it — while X misses the pipeline-resolution-order issue entirely despite it being the PR's most-discussed bug. X's findings (docstring staleness tagged 'major', an unverifiable claim about a missing try/except, minor naming nitpicks) are lower-value and less grounded, whereas Y's extra findings (UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES skip path, cache invalidation scope, status_code fragility) are specific, calibrated, and tied to actual code paths. Both give concrete file/line pointers, so actionability is comparably strong; neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
